claims.claim: Resolution 0000108/2015 adopts CCP-2014 for INVÍAS National Road Network bridge studies, design, construction, and maintenance.
The resolution is effective on publication and repeals contrary provisions, especially Resolution 3600/1996.
The general design hazard is 7% probability of exceedance in 75 years, approximately a 1000-year mean return period, at 5% damping.
The general procedure obtains PGA, Ss, and S1 from the three maps or contracting-entity-approved special values/maps and uses linear contour interpolation.
A finite official selectable locality count and exact locality-to-PGA/Ss/S1 registry is available in the published package.
claims.locator: Official ZIP central directory and C3.10.2.1

claims.claim: The site-specific procedure is mandatory for distance under 10 km to an active fault, class F, expected long-duration events, or bridge importance requiring lower exceedance probability/longer return period.
The site-specific analysis must document uncertainty and be reviewed/approved by experts appointed by the contracting entity.
A site-specific spectrum cannot be below two-thirds of the general spectrum from 0.5Tr to 2.0Tr.
Site classes A through F and class-F site-specific requirements are defined by 3.10.3.1.
Fpga, Fa, and Fv values and linear interpolation rules are provided in Tables 3.10.3.2-1 through 3.10.3.2-3.
As = Fpga * PGA.
SDS = Fa * Ss.
SD1 = Fv * S1.
claims.locator: Section 3 physical page 61, printed 3-57; equation mislabeled 3.10.4.2-3 and referenced as 3.10.4.2-6 in 3.10.6

claims.claim: Ts = SD1 / SDS.
claims.locator: Section 3 physical page 60, printed 3-56, definition and Figure 3.10.4.1-1

claims.claim: T0 has one unambiguous value.
The spectrum uses a linear initial branch, SDS plateau, and SD1/T long-period branch; no 1/T^2 branch is specified.
claims.locator: Section 3 physical pages 60-61, printed 3-56 and 3-57, Equations 3.10.4.2-1, -4, -5

claims.claim: The contracting entity assigns critical, essential, or other bridge category.
Seismic performance zones 1-4 are selected by SD1 thresholds 0.15, 0.30, and 0.50 with the printed boundary inclusions.
Substructure and connection R factors are tabulated by bridge category/element.
claims.locator: Section 3 physical page 62, printed 3-58, Tables 3.10.7.1-1 and 3.10.7.1-2

claims.claim: R applies in both orthogonal horizontal directions; inelastic time-history analysis uses R=1 for substructure and all connections.
An adopted Section 3 amendment or erratum supersedes the recovered passages.
The April 2026 Section 13 consultation is an adopted amendment affecting Section 3.
